daily_brief: The escalating US-Iran conflict dominated global headlines as President Trump threatened to strike Iranian bridges and power plants next week while reimposing a naval blockade, with US strikes killing at least seven Iranian military personnel [BBC World, RFI, The Hindu]. Iran retaliated with attacks on US bases in the region and threatened to close additional strategic seaways including the Bab el-Mandeb Strait [The Hindu, RFI]. China reported its weakest quarterly economic growth in over three years at 4.3%, missing Beijing's target as the Iran war's disruption of oil shipping compounds domestic challenges including a property slump and weak consumer spending [BBC World, France 24, Financial Times]. In Europe, Commission President von der Leyen arrived in Kyiv to announce new defence initiatives as a Russian strike killed three in Odesa, while Ukraine claimed strikes on 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea [France 24]. Spain defeated France 2-0 to reach the 2026 World Cup final in a tactical masterclass that ended Didier Deschamps' tenure as France manager [RFI, France 24, Al Jazeera]. Meanwhile, a second American Ebola patient has been evacuated to Germany for treatment as the DRC outbreak continues to expand with over 1,900 confirmed cases [The Guardian].

top_stories.headline: US Escalates Iran Conflict: Trump Threatens Power Plants and Bridges as Strikes Continue
bullets: US forces carried out strikes on Iran for a fourth consecutive day, killing at least seven Iranian military personnel at an army barracks near Iranshahr
Trump reversed his 20% toll threat on Strait of Hormuz shipping but reimposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports effective Tuesday evening
Trump warned in a Fox News interview that strikes would target power plants and bridges next week if Iran does not return to negotiations
Iran retaliated with drone attacks on a US military base in Jordan and struck targets in Bahrain and Kuwait, while threatening to close additional strategic waterways

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's retreat on Hormuz tolls as sign he is struggling to end the war; noted UN human rights chief's previous warning that targeting civilian infrastructure is a war crime

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on military developments and Trump's ultimatum

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iran's IRGC threat to close 'all other export corridors' and potential Houthi involvement in Bab el-Mandeb

top_stories.headline: China's Economy Grows at Weakest Pace in Over Three Years, Missing Government Target
bullets: GDP expanded 4.3% year-on-year in Q2 2026, below the 4.5-5% target and the slowest growth since Q4 2022
Strong exports driven by global AI demand were offset by the Iran war's disruption to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz
Domestic challenges persist including a years-long property sector crisis and weak consumer spending
New home prices contracted for another month, falling 0.1% in June

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran war impact on oil prices as key external factor

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted AI and EV exports as bright spots amid broader weakness

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline emphasized historic nature of low growth rates

top_stories.headline: Von der Leyen Visits Kyiv with Defence Plans as Russia Strikes Odesa, Killing Three
bullets: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen arrived in Ukraine to announce new initiatives deepening EU-Ukraine defence integration
A Russian strike on Odesa killed three civilians and hospitalized three others, damaging residential buildings
Russia confirmed targeting port infrastructure in Odesa and Chornomorsk, hitting fuel storage and vessels
Ukraine claimed overnight strikes on 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea including gas tankers

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with von der Leyen visit and new defence initiatives

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on Ukraine expanding attacks to Sea of Azov as strategic development

top_stories.headline: Spain Defeats France 2-0 to Reach 2026 World Cup Final
bullets: Spain controlled the semifinal in Dallas, with France failing to register a shot on target until after the 75th minute
Mikel Oyarzabal converted a penalty after Lucas Digne fouled Lamine Yamal; Pedro Porro added a second goal
France's highly-touted attack of Mbappé, Olise, and Dembélé was neutralized by Spain's midfield trio of Rodri, Olmo, and Ruiz
Didier Deschamps will end his tenure as France manager after the third-place playoff on Saturday

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Straightforward match reporting emphasizing Spain's composure

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: French perspective noting end of 'French dream' of third consecutive final

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Spain's tactical masterclass and Deschamps' tactical failures

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on Deschamps' disappointment and Mbappé's criticism of France's tactics

top_stories.headline: Second American Ebola Patient Evacuated to Germany as DRC Outbreak Expands
bullets: A US humanitarian worker in his 60s contracted Ebola in Bunia, DRC and was transferred to Frankfurt's university hospital for treatment
The Trump administration blocked American citizens in the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights
The outbreak has recorded over 1,900 confirmed cases and more than 700 deaths from the Bundibugyo strain, for which no vaccine or approved treatment exists
First patients have been enrolled in a record-breaking treatment trial just six weeks after WHO declared the outbreak a public health emergency

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed context on outbreak scale and treatment trial progress; noted worker was employed by Samaritan's Purse

additional_stories.headline: France's National Assembly expected to give final approval to assisted dying legislation after years of debate
Seven Britons among 13 killed in deadly wildfires in Spain's Almería province
Hungary's parliament votes to remove President Tamás Sulyok from office using constitutional amendment
New Zealand detects first case of H5N1 bird flu in migratory seabird found on Wellington beach
ICE suspends most vehicle stops nationwide after agents fatally shot two men in Maine and Texas
Taiwan orders dozens of sea drones with US technology to counter China's expanding maritime operations
India-UK trade agreement takes effect, eliminating tariffs on 96.8% of UK tariff lines immediately
French PM Lecornu leads delegation of 12 ministers to Morocco for first high-level meeting since 2019
Stripe and Advent make $53 billion bid for PayPal
Australia unveils AI standards for data center construction and model training
